tasks.implementation_summary: Added structural report scope schemas, structural change scope key validation, change-scoped schemas for adv-researcher, adv-tron, and adv-scanner-bundle, and exported all new schemas/types. Kept current SupportedSubagentReportSchema and task.subagent_reports task-scoped for existing consumers, while adding ScopedSubagentReportSchema for all variants. Task-scoped engineer/reviewer schemas now accept structural task scope objects and legacy non-empty scope strings to preserve backward compatibility. Expanded schema tests to cover optimized handoff variants, agent/scope pairing rejection, scope-key formats, and packet anchors. Verification: pnpm exec vitest run src/types/subagent-reports.test.ts passed 21 tests; pnpm exec vitest run src/types/subagent-reports.test.ts src/adv-engineer-assets.test.ts src/adv-reviewer-asset.test.ts passed 124 tests; pnpm run typecheck passed; pnpm run format:check passed.

Broadened subagentReportSubmittedSignal payload to carry ScopedSubagentReportSchema and optional taskId, while applySubagentReportSubmittedToState now persists every report to sidecar, mirrors task-scoped reports to legacy task.subagent_reports[], accepts taskless change-scoped optimized handoffs, and dedupes using legacy task keys for task reports plus scope-aware keys for change reports. subagentReportKey now preserves the legacy (changeId|taskId|agent|attempt) shape when taskId is present and uses structural scope only for taskless/change reports. Verification covered pure mutation, signal-handler compatibility, typecheck, and format.

wisdom.content: When the OpenCode session is launched from the canonical checkout, ADV state mutations such as adv_task_update can hit the trunk-write isolation guard even after adv_worktree_resume. Route the mutation with target_path set to the resumed ADV worktree and explicit target confirmation.

When introducing new sub-agent report variants, keep the current task-consumer schema (`SupportedSubagentReportSchema` / `SubagentReportSchema`) task-scoped until persistence/readback consumers migrate; expose a separate all-variant schema (`ScopedSubagentReportSchema`) for new optimized handoff validation. This preserves typecheck and legacy task.subagent_reports[] compatibility.

For report sidecar migration, preserve legacy task report idempotency keys whenever taskId is present (`changeId|taskId|agent|attempt`) and use scope-aware keys only for taskless/change-scoped reports. This keeps old Temporal histories replay-safe while enabling new sidecar scopes.

For taskless report submit, compute source identity from structural scope and omit `taskId` from the signal payload; task-scoped reports keep taskId for legacy consumers. Follow-up agenda descriptions should include change/scope/agent/attempt plus task id when present.

During sidecar migrations, opt-in readback should merge canonical sidecar entries with legacy embedded entries and dedupe by the same report key. Compatibility checks such as checkpoint legacy-output suppression must also consult sidecar task-scoped reports, not only task.subagent_reports[].

For stale enforcement-doc cleanup, add asset tests against live orchestration docs rather than only searching manually. Keep historical CHANGELOG references allowed, but block active ADV_INSTRUCTIONS/.opencode agent claims and repository maps for retired directories.

When adding optimized handoff reports, pin both agent prompt transport and command packet anchors in one asset test. Validate report-specific anchors via getSubagentReportPacketAnchors so schema field-source changes break prompt/packet tests deterministically.
Final `pnpm run check` can fail only at Prettier after targeted tests pass. For new asset tests, run `pnpm run format` before final check/build to avoid a formatting-only verification retry.

Command packet examples for strict report transports should show a complete schema-valid payload skeleton, not only agent/phase/summary fields; otherwise prompt text can drift even when anchors exist.

acceptance.approval_evidence: User approved acceptance with inline reply: approve. Verification passed: pnpm run check, pnpm test, pnpm run build; independent reviewer READY; contract review matrix 22/22 pass/respected.

items.text: `adv_subagent_report_submit` accepts strict persisted optimized handoff reports for `adv-researcher`, `adv-tron`, and scanner-bundle lanes.
Existing `adv-engineer` / `adv-reviewer` report behavior stays backward-compatible.
Taskless reports are queryable with explicit source metadata; final readback shape is chosen by design for cleanest stack fit.
Scanner persistence uses orchestrator-submitted aggregate bundles; individual scanners keep no ADV tool access.
Persisted report `follow_ups[]` create bounded, source-tagged agenda items.
Harden inspects report-created agenda items and fixes those that are safe, adjacent, and campsite/touched-scope applicable; non-applicable items get rationale.
All `enforceTaskPolicy` / stale `guards/` references are corrected, removed, or explicitly historical.
Specs, agent prompts, command packets, and tests lock schema variants, packet anchors, agenda behavior, readback shape, and malformed-report rejection.
Preserve strict Zod validation at the ingest boundary.
Preserve existing durable report semantics for `adv-engineer` and `adv-reviewer`.
Keep the main ADV agent as orchestrator; sub-agents do not complete gates or mutate orchestration state.
Do not claim runtime enforcement for built-in `task` dispatch unless it is actually implemented.
Prefer compact optimized handoff payloads over raw transcript persistence.
Keep agenda follow-up generation bounded and source-tagged.
Harden inspection must remain limited to safe, adjacent, campsite/touched-scope-applicable items.
Do not weaken strict report validation or rely on LLM-parsed prose as the only persistence path.
Do not allow sub-agents to complete gates, create changes, or own orchestration decisions.
Do not introduce fake runtime enforcement claims without real implementation and tests.
Do not make scanner persistence so verbose that it defeats context-purity goals.
Do not create unbounded agenda noise.
Do not require harden to fix non-adjacent or unrelated agenda items.
Do not expand into broad ADV latency remediation beyond report readback needs.

rows.evidence: subagent-report + schema tests cover strict researcher/tron/scanner-bundle variants.
schema/report/reviewer asset tests passed; task-scoped aliases and legacy behavior retained.
change/checkpoint tests passed; include.subagentReports sidecar readback merges scoped/legacy reports with metadata.
optimized-handoff asset test passed; review/harden define orchestrator SCANNER_BUNDLE_REPORT and scanners do not submit reports.
subagent-report tests passed; follow_ups capped and source-tagged as subagent-followup agenda items.
optimized-handoff asset test passed; adv-harden has Report-Created Agenda Audit with fix/rationale handling.
stale-enforcement asset test passed; no live enforceTaskPolicy/runtime-enforced refs outside tests.
optimized-handoff, spec asset, schema, and tool tests passed; packet anchors and malformed-report rejection covered.
Strict Zod schemas and INVALID_REPORT path remain in subagent-report tool.
SupportedSubagentReportSchema remains task-scoped and allows legacy string scope.
Agents only submit own reports; adv-tron blocks change/task/gate mutations; orchestrator owns bundles/gates.
Live docs now say agent-self-enforced/no runtime guard; stale-enforcement test guards it.
Optimized report schemas persist compact sources/evidence/findings/summary; readback is opt-in.
consumeFollowUps caps to MAX_REPORT_FOLLOW_UPS and writes Source metadata.
adv-harden Report-Created Agenda Audit requires safe+adjacent+campsite/touched-scope to fix; otherwise rationale/drift stop.
Reviewer verdict READY; strict tool-call transport required.
Reviewer verdict READY; sub-agents do not complete gates/create changes/own decisions.
False runtime-enforcement claims removed and guarded by tests.
Scanner persistence is aggregate bundle with bounded payload skeleton.
follow_ups capped and source-tagged; tests cover agenda creation.
adv-harden explicitly does not require non-adjacent/unrelated agenda fixes; rationale path required.
Touched scope limited to report schemas/persistence/readback/tooling/prompts/specs/tests and harden agenda handling.

acceptanceCriteria: `adv_subagent_report_submit` accepts strict persisted optimized handoff reports for `adv-researcher`, `adv-tron`, and scanner-bundle lanes.
Taskless reports are queryable with explicit source metadata; final readback shape is chosen by design for cleanest stack fit.
Scanner persistence uses orchestrator-submitted aggregate bundles; individual scanners keep no ADV tool access.
Persisted report `follow_ups[]` create bounded, source-tagged agenda items.
Harden inspects report-created agenda items and fixes those that are safe, adjacent, and campsite/touched-scope applicable; non-applicable items get rationale.
All `enforceTaskPolicy` / stale `guards/` references are corrected, removed, or explicitly historical.
Specs, agent prompts, command packets, and tests lock schema variants, packet anchors, agenda behavior, readback shape, and malformed-report rejection.
